name: Fertilizer / Urea
summary: Day 73-74 (Mon-Tue). **CF INDUSTRIES +8.2% 1D TO $124.48 = MOVE OF THE DAY.** Sector regime shift: CF +8.2%, Nutrien +4.3%, BASF +3.5%, Linde +2.3% all bid together = NOT a single-stock event. **CAUSAL CHAIN (~85% crisis-linked): (1) Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad address asked Indians to CUT FERTILIZER USE ~50% (mixed organic) — one of seven 'consumption-restraint' appeals; market reads this as Indian-government PUBLIC CONFIRMATION that fertilizer-import shock is BINDING, not hypothetical. (2) Qatar/Ras Laffan fertilizer/ammonia complexes (QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients) curtailed since Feb 28 attacks; ~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected. (3) India's IPL DAP tender May 11 received offers at $935/t cfr east coast / $930/t cfr west coast — EXTRAORDINARY prints; DAP normally trades $580-650 ex-crisis. (4) Urea FOB US Gulf futures +50% since war began; UBS raised CF target $97→$140 explicitly noting 'may not fully reflect severity of ongoing industry disruptions.' (5) CF Q1 2026: net earnings $615M, EBITDA $983M confirm pricing-power capture; YTD shares now +71%.** Secondary contributor: Wheat +3.2% / Corn +3.2% same session = fertilizer cost pass-through to grain producers + tender activity. **This is a SUPPLY-DISRUPTION + DEMAND-CONFIRMATION confluence for the first time in cycle. REVERSIBILITY LOW: Ras Laffan not coming back inside 60-day window even under ceasefire; QAFCO production scarring structural; India's import bill binding through monsoon planting season (June-Aug).** The fertilizer trade is the cleanest crisis-linked long if Brent stays $100+ through Q3. **NEW PROPOSED EDGE: fertilizer-urea → india (existing edge, STRENGTHEN from weight ~5-6 to 7.5-8.0 on activation today via Modi address + DAP tender + US Gulf urea +50%).**

current.price: CF Industries $124.48 (+8.2% 1D May 12); Nutrien $71.29 (+4.3% 1D); Linde $504.40 (+2.3%); BASF €53.46 (+3.5%); DAP India CFR $935/t east / $930/t west (May 11 IPL tender lowest offers); Urea US Gulf FOB futures ~$520-550/t (+50% vs pre-war baseline)
current.delta_1m: CF +2.6%; NTR -3.3%; Linde +0.2%; BASF +4.1%; Wheat +10.3%; Corn +8.0%; Palm Oil +11.4%; Rough Rice +10.2%; Urea US Gulf +50% vs pre-war
current.structural_drivers: Qatar Ras Laffan offline = NOT 30-day disruption but structural input-cost reset (QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients curtailed); 35-40% of global urea export capacity affected; India's IPL DAP tender at $935/t = extraordinary (normal $580-650); Modi government PUBLIC CONFIRMATION of binding shock via consumption-restraint appeal; UBS CF PT $97→$140 with note that target 'may not fully reflect severity'; Reversibility LOW: not inside 60-day window; cleanest crisis-linked long if Brent stays $100+ through Q3.

India urea stocks fall below 40 LMT by May 1, 2026
DAP India CFR remains above $900/t through end of monsoon planting (Aug 2026) — confirms structural input-cost reset

active_triggers.condition: Indian fertilizer subsidy bill 2026-27 budget revision (rumored to overshoot ₹1.95 lakh cr) within 30 days — confirms binding fiscal cost on India

CAUSAL CHAIN (~85% crisis-linked): (1) Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad address asked Indians to cut fertilizer use ~50% — Indian-government PUBLIC CONFIRMATION binding shock, not hypothetical. (2) Qatar/Ras Laffan QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients curtailed since Feb 28 = ~35-40% global urea export capacity affected. (3) IPL DAP tender May 11: offers $935/t cfr east / $930/t cfr west — EXTRAORDINARY (normal $580-650). (4) Urea US Gulf FOB futures +50% since war began. recent_signals.headline: CF -3.1% MAY 8 ON BLOWOUT EARNINGS — SELL-THE-NEWS AFTER +54% YTD RUN. CF Q1 EPS $3.98 vs $2.50 est (+59% surprise); revenue $1.99B vs $1.8B; net earnings $615M; EBITDA $983M; 99% available ammonia capacity utilization Q1. Urea Yuzhny FOB $616.25/T (+6.71% May 6, +15.12% MoM, +81.69% YTD); DAP $722.50/T (+11.58% MoM). **Hormuz handles ~35% of global seaborne fertilizer trade; Cape rerouting +10-14 days; China sulfuric-acid export suspension from May 2026 = structural phosphate bottleneck = nitrogen/phosphate fundamentals STRONGER than CF earnings showed.** Smart-money rotation: fertilizer-already-up (CF, NTR) → fertilizer-supply-chain (BASF +1.5%, Linde flat, Indian fertilizer producers as proxy). Most quantitatively interesting move on May 8 tape: signals fertilizer-Hormuz-cascade trade is TIRED at this entry level despite operational reality continuing to support higher prices.
sources: intel dossier Section E + market dossier C5

recent_signals.headline: CF -6.5% / NTR -4.4% — APRIL 17 PLAYBOOK RERUN: MOU 'BOTH SIDES LIFT HORMUZ BLOCKADES' = QATAR FERTILIZER NORMALIZATION EXPECTATION = SCARCITY-PREMIUM UNWIND. CF -10.5% 1M now tracking symmetrical inverse of Hormuz-closure scarcity rally that drove it +27% in 3M. Urea generic $577.50/T (May 5) drifting lower; Argus Q2 forecast $715 has room to compress further; Egypt NCIC late-April spot $852/t = high-water mark. Second-cleanest peace-dividend trade after energy. If MOU signs: CF could give back another 8-12%. If Iran rejects: CF bounces +6-9% within 48hr.
